key_insight: NIMO is an open-source platform that decouples AI planning from lab robotics, letting diverse closed-loop materials discovery workflows run through a simple, flexible interface.
engineering_relevance: It lowers integration effort across heterogeneous labs by using CSV-based communication, a discrete candidate-pool design, and a unified Python API with multiple built-in optimization algorithms.
method: Modular AI and robot layers communicate through simple CSV file exchange.
A discrete candidate-pool architecture encodes domain knowledge for experiment selection.
The platform ships with 12 AI algorithms plus a no-code desktop app for human-in-the-loop use.
evidence_signal: The paper reviews six SDL implementations using NIMO across electrolyte discovery, organic synthesis, thin films, fuel-cell informatics, coffee-ring phase exploration, and legacy liquid-handling automation, including interoperability with IvoryOS.
watch_item: Because the platform relies on standardized file exchange and multiple integrations, real-world performance may depend on how well each lab’s hardware, data format, and workflow are configured.
paper_title: NIMO: A Software Platform for Closed-Loop Materials Exploration with Diverse AI Algorithms
